title: Future return accidentally used as model feature
StandardScaler fit_transform runs before chronological split
code:
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
scaled = StandardScaler().fit_transform(df[['close_usd', 'volume_usd']])
train = scaled[:6]
test = scaled[6:]

title: Random split used for financial time series
code:
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
train, test = train_test_split(df, test_size=0.25, shuffle=True, random_state=7)

title: Transaction cost variable is declared but never applied
code:
transaction_cost_bps = 10
df['position'] = (df['close_usd'].pct_change().shift(1) > 0).astype(int)
df['strategy_return'] = df['position'] * df['close_usd'].pct_change()
sharpe = df['strategy_return'].mean() / df['strategy_return'].std()

title: Report claims state-of-the-art performance without baseline
code: The agentic strategy is clearly state-of-the-art and beats all alternatives. We omit baseline comparisons because the equity curve is visually compelling.

title: Simple shifted momentum with explicit costs
code:
df['ret_1d'] = df['close_usd'].pct_change()
df['signal'] = (df['ret_1d'].rolling(3).mean().shift(1) > 0).astype(int)
df['turnover'] = df['signal'].diff().abs().fillna(0)
df['strategy_return'] = df['signal'].shift(1) * df['ret_1d'] - 0.001 * df['turnover']

title: Transaction-cost notebook shifts turnover to execution date
code:
# Source: Intro_Transaction_Costs.ipynb cell 13
turnover = abs(vol_signal.diff())
# Shift by -1 to get the date at which the transaction occurred, which was at close yesterday.
turnover = turnover.shift(-1)
brokerage = turnover * brokerage_fees

title: QuoteMedia strategy selects future winners before trading
code:
prices = prices.sort_values(['ticker', 'date'])
prices['future_ret'] = prices.groupby('ticker')['adj_close'].pct_change().shift(-1)
prices['signal'] = (prices.groupby('date')['future_ret'].rank(pct=True) > 0.8).astype(int)
prices['strategy_return'] = prices['signal'] * prices.groupby('ticker')['adj_close'].pct_change()

title: QuoteMedia scaler is fit inside the training window
code:
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
prices = prices.sort_values(['ticker', 'date'])
train = prices[prices['date'] < '2022-10-01'].copy()
test = prices[prices['date'] >= '2022-10-01'].copy()
scaler = StandardScaler().fit(train[['adj_close', 'volume']])
train[['scaled_price', 'scaled_volume']] = scaler.transform(train[['adj_close', 'volume']])
test[['scaled_price', 'scaled_volume']] = scaler.transform(test[['adj_close', 'volume']])
